_paths_comment: Portable paths: ${HOUFIN_DATA}/${HOUFIN_PROCESSED} expand at load (src/config_utils.py; default <repo>/data and <repo>/data/processed locally, override via env on HPC). NOTE: the DESK/encoder pipeline is a later milestone; this layout is provisional and its stream wiring (2-stream state_*_bio_ema10 -> N-stream state_*) is still to be reconciled.

states._ema_comment: ema_tau is a LIGHT backward-EMA time constant (~2 yr, half-life ~1.4 yr) applied at state-build time -- just denoises annual weather so the per-year covariate grid is a near-instantaneous suitability signal. The DECADAL demographic lag is NOT here; it lives on the DESK OUTPUT (learned output-EMA, desk.output_ema). Was 10 (~7-yr half-life); reduced so z_raw fluxes and the two lags stay identifiable.

esk._spacetime_comment: Joint spatiotemporal ESK (bbs_mode=trend, run_spacetime_esk): Ruzicka kernel-PCA over the trend-based annual community points. landmark_mode='diverse' guarantees coverage of occupied decade x spatial-tile x abundance-quantile strata, then fills the remaining budget uniformly so rare manifold regions are represented without discarding population weighting. spatial_bins is per axis. 'random' remains a uniform ablation; 'stratified' is proportional by decade. sigma=0.0: annual points (n_weeks=1), no weekly axis. Writes esk/spacetime/{Z.npy recent grid, valid_mask, esk_landmarks, esk_projmat, meta.json}.

_trend_comment: Trend-product spatiotemporal community target (trend_community.build_trend_points). Applies published %/yr trends to the 2023 eBird annual anchor, blending eBird recent (2012-2022) and BBS long-term (1966-2022) rates with a smooth handoff, integrated backward to first_year. Paths (bbs_trend_grid, ebird_trend_grid, community_trend_list) fall back to data_config; ebird_weekly_grid falls back to paths.ebird_folder.

trend._anchor_comment: anchor_mode=trends-abd (default): the reference-year abundance E is the eBird trends product's own midpoint reference `abd` (rel-abundance at (start_year+end_year)/2, ~2017), forward-extrapolated to anchor_year along abd_ppy: E = abd*(1+abd_ppy/100)^(anchor_year-mid). Same product+cells as the rate (no coverage mismatch); needs NO weekly eBird download. anchor_mode=weekly is the legacy path (2023 annual mean of the weekly status rasters; needs the projected weekly grid + --require-weekly download). anchor_year=2025 is the reference year (end of the covariate timeline); reconstruction compounds backward from it. Both abd_ppy (eBird) and tr{AOU} (BBS) are percent-PER-YEAR.
trend._stride_comment: point_year_stride=1: emit EVERY year 1966-2025, so the ESK basis input and the DESK per-year targets are fully spatiotemporal (not just endpoints). ESK landmark subsampling keeps the Nystrom eigh bounded.
trend._cap_comment: TWO overlapping soft caps on the backward change (both C1-smooth tanh: identity below the knee, saturating toward the max). (1) RELATIVE/fold cap on log(N/E): soft_knee_fold=10 -> soft_max_fold=100; protects rare-base cells. (2) ABSOLUTE cap on (N-E) in per-species units = multiples of the species' p99 present abundance (its 'best-habitat' ceiling): abs_soft_knee_mult=0.3 (change up to ~1/3 of the ceiling passes) -> abs_soft_max_mult=1.0 (change saturates near the ceiling, i.e. at most ~worst-cell -> best-cell). Neither is community-relative. smooth_sigma_cells>0 adds a post-cap masked Gaussian on log-abundance (in cells) so a capped extreme can't poison neighbours (0 = off).
trend._coverage_comment: Per-cell, per-year coverage gate: emit a historical (cell,year) community point only if >= min_coverage of the cell's occupying species have a trend informing that year (eBird when the blend leans recent, BBS when deep). Prevents false-stability points where most species would be held constant (~23% of cells have <50% deep BBS coverage). The recent anchor year is always kept.

bbs._comment: Community + ESK point settings. bbs_mode 'off'|'validate'|'trend' via the top-level bbs_mode above. community_top_n, z_dir (where trend_community writes the ESK point files) and n_landmarks are the live keys under bbs_mode=trend; the smoothing/anomaly knobs below belong to the deprecated amplitude path.
bbs._smooth_comment: Gaussian NW smoothing in GRID CELLS (25 km). sigma_s=2 (~50 km stdev, ~100 km 2-sigma reach) connects nearby BBS routes without continental blur; the raw-BBS diagnostic showed sigma_s=5 kept only ~65% of real per-cell change vs ~78% at sigma_s=2. Coverage is now handled by the relative support floor, so sigma_s no longer needs to be large. No expansion-front concern: House Finch (the transfer target) is excluded from the community.
bbs._ablation_comment: Suppression knobs still at the max-permissive ablation values (pseudocount 0.1, shrink_k 0, cap ln100, sigma_t 1) so this run isolates the bbs_community species fix + sigma_s reduction against the prior ablation run. Restore moderate robustness (shrink_k~1, cap ln10, pseudocount 0.5) for a production config once attribution is settled.
bbs._n_landmarks_comment: Landmark budget for the joint-ESK Nystrom eigendecomposition. 16000 (was 30000): the 30k x 30k eigh OOM'd the A100 and fell back to CPU (~44 min). 16000^2 float64 ~= 2 GB fits the GPU with headroom.

desk._label_year_comment: label_year = the anchor/reference year (matches trend.anchor_year). The Ružicka metric is anchored on the reconstructed year-label_year community, and the output-EMA window runs [output_ema.warmup_start .. label_year].
desk._train_comment: epochs is an UPPER BOUND; early stopping on held-out Stab(val) (patience) restores the best weights, so a long budget is safe. 100 epochs left every loss still descending (undertrained) -> raised to 500. batch_years = reconstruction grids/step; enrich ALSO forwards the 2023 grid + all ~11 point-year grids every step (full-batch supervised), so GPU mem ~ (12+batch_years) whole grids through a 512-wide MLP with gradients. 8 fits a 40GB A100; 32 OOMs. Bigger batch_years buys ~nothing here (supervised term is already full-batch) -- the win is epochs, not batch.
desk._spatial_conv_comment: Tight residual masked (partial) conv on the latent grid: injects neighbourhood context a point-wise encoder cannot learn. gamma inits 0 so training starts as the exact point-wise, ESK-aligned model. kernel=3 => 3x3 (one 25km ring).
desk._trend_comment: bbs_mode=trend: DESK predicts per-year z_raw from (lightly-smoothed) covariates, applies a LEARNED causal output-EMA over the year axis (output_ema) to get z_ema, and supervises z_ema against the trend-based spatiotemporal z-target -- every (cell,year) point EQUAL weight (uniform_stab). The output-EMA models demographic lag: the year-Y community is a leaky integral of past suitability, so per-year predictions flux with the environment while the supervised decadal estimate is a stable mixture of recent years. holdout_frac of eBird cells withheld for honest z-space eval.
desk._output_ema_comment: Learned causal EMA over the year axis on the PREDICTED z (not the inputs). half_life learned in half_life_bounds (yr) via a sigmoid reparam; alpha=1-2^(-1/half_life); z_ema(Y)=alpha*z_raw(Y)+(1-alpha)*z_ema(Y-1), scanned forward from warmup_start. Years < 1966 are unsupervised but seed the EMA. Per-year gradient checkpointing keeps the ~84-year window in A100 memory. The learned half_life is saved to desk_meta and reused by build_final_z_cube.
desk._enrich_comment: enrich (bbs_mode='enrich'): supervise DESK at historical (cell,year) points against the BBS-amplitude community projected into the eBird ESK basis (z_obs). ebird_loss_fraction=0.8 => modern eBird contributes 80% of the stabilizing loss regardless of the ~10:1 BBS point-count advantage (eBird is the reliable anchor). holdout_frac of eBird cells are held out entirely for honest z-space reconstruction eval.
desk._zdir_comment: enrich uses the JOINT spatiotemporal ESK basis (esk/spacetime), not the eBird-only sigma_1.0 basis, so Z can represent historical change directions. latent_dim 32 = full joint basis (expressiveness). For bbs_mode=validate/off, point z_dir back at esk/sigma_1.0 and latent_dim 16.

latent_cube._radius_comment: Gap-fill footprint in KM; build_final_z_cube converts to pixels at the model grid (radius_km/res_km), so it is resolution-independent. 100 km -> ~4 px at 27 km.
